title: daphne kamtschatica subsp. jezoensis
intro: Daphne kamtschatica subsp. jezoensis, also known as Jeju daphne or Japanese daphne, is a subspecies of the evergreen shrub Daphne kamtschatica, native to Korea and Japan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues. The health benefits of Daphne kamtschatica subsp. jezoensis include its ability to boost the immune system, improve circulation, and act as an antioxidant, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Daphne kamtschatica subsp. jezoensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
